volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 5.3.1013b - Section 5.5.1066)

sections.label: Section 5.3.1013b
sections.translation: Certainly .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1013c
sections.original: Non vides quantum mali ex ea re excites ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you see how much evil you will be causing by that course ?— He suspects himself to be a foundling .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1014b
sections.translation: Do you say so ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1014c
sections.original: Certe sic erit , inquam , mi vir .
sections.translation: Assuredly it will be so .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1015
sections.translation: Admit it .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1015b
sections.original: Au obsecro te , istuc inimicis siet .
sections.translation: Hold now — prithee , let that be for our enemies . Am I to admit that he is not my son who really is ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1017
sections.original: Quid ? metuis ne non , quum velis , convincas esse illum tuum ?
sections.translation: What ! are you afraid that you can not prove that he is yours , whenever you please ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1018
sections.translation: Because my daughter has been found ? [*]

sections.label: Section 5.3.1018b
sections.original: Non ; sed , quod magis credendum siet ,
sections.translation: No ; but for a reason why it should be much sooner believed — because he is just like you in disposition ,

sections.label: Section 5.3.1020
sections.original: Convincas facile ex te natum ; nam tui similis est probe ;
sections.translation: you will easily prove that he is your child ; for he is exactly like you ; why , he has not a single vice left him but you have just the same . Then , besides , no woman could have been the mother of such a son but yourself . But he ’ s coming out of doors , and how demure ! When you understand the matter , you may form your own conclusions .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1024
sections.original: Si unquam ullum fuit tempus , mater , quum ego voluptati tibi
sections.translation: If there ever was any time , mother , when I caused you pleasure ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.1025
sections.original: Fuerim , dictus filius tuus tua voluntate , obsecro ,
sections.translation: being called your son by your own desire , I beseech : you to remember it , and now to take compassion on me in my distress . A thing I beg and request — do discover to me my parents .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1028
sections.original: Obsecro , mi gnate , ne istuc in animum inducas tuum ,
sections.translation: I conjure you , my son , not to entertain that notion in your mind , that you are another person ’ s child .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1029b
sections.translation: I am .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1029c
sections.translation: Wretch that I am ! ( Turning to CHREMES .) Was it this that you wanted , pray ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1030
sections.original: Ita mihi atque huic sis superstes ut ex me atque ex hoc natus es .
sections.translation: ( To CLITIPHO .) So may you be the survivor of me and of him , you are my son and his ; and henceforth , if you love me , take care that I never hear that speech from you again .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1032
sections.original: At ego , si me metuis , mores cave in te esse istos sentiam .
sections.translation: But I say , if you fear me , take care how I find these propensities existing in you .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1033
sections.translation: What propensities ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1033b
sections.original: Si scire vis , ego dicam : gerro , iners , fraus , heluo ,
sections.translation: If you wish to know , I ’ ll tell you ; being a trifler , an idler , a cheat , a glutton , a debauchee , a spendthrift — Believe me , and believe that you are our son .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1035
sections.translation: This is not the language of a parent .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1035b
sections.original: Non si ex capite sis meo
sections.translation: If you had been born from my head , Clitipho , just as they say Minerva was from Jove ’ s , none the more on that account would I suffer myself to be disgraced by your profligacy . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.4.1038
sections.translation: May the Gods forbid it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1038b
sections.original: Deos nescio : ego quod potero sedulo .
sections.translation: I don ’ t know as to the Gods ; [*] so far as I shall be enabled , I will carefully prevent it . You are seeking that which you possess — parents ; that which you are in want of you don ’ t seek —

sections.label: Section 5.4.1040
sections.original: Quomodo obsequare , et ut serves quod labore invenerit .
sections.translation: in what way to pay obedience to a father , and to preserve what he acquired by his industry . That you by trickery should bring before my eyes — I am ashamed to mention the unseemly word in her presence ( pointing to SOSTRATA ) , but you were not in any degree ashamed to act thus .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1043b
sections.original: Eheu , quam ego nunc totus displiceo mihi ,
sections.translation: Alas ! how thoroughly displeased I now am with myself ! How much ashamed ! nor do I know how to make a beginning to pacify him .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1045
sections.original: Enimvero Chremes nimis graviter cruciat adolescentulum ,
sections.translation: Why really , Chremes is treating his son too harshly and too unkindly . I ’ m come out , therefore , to make peace between them . Most opportunely I see them both .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1047b
sections.original: Ehem , Menedeme , cur non arcessi iubes
sections.translation: Well , Menedemus , why don ’ t you order my daughter to be sent for , and close with the offer [*] of the portion that I mentioned ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.1048b
sections.translation: My husband , I entreat you not to do it .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1049b
sections.translation: Father , I entreat you to forgive me .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1049c
sections.translation: Forgive him , Chremes ;

sections.label: Section 5.5.1050
sections.translation: do let then prevail upon you .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1050b
sections.original: Egon mea bona ut dem Bacchidi dono sciens ?
sections.translation: Am I knowingly to make my property a present to Bacchis ? I ’ ll not do it .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1051b
sections.translation: Why , we would not suffer it .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1051c
sections.translation: If you desire me to live , father , do forgive me .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1052b
sections.translation: Do , my dear Chremes .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1052c
sections.original: Age quaeso ne tam offirma te , Chreme .
sections.translation: Come , Chremes , pray , don ’ t be so obdurate .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1053
sections.original: Quid istic ? video non licere ut coeperam hoc pertendere .
sections.translation: What am I to do here ? I see I am not allowed to carry this through , as I had intended .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1054
sections.translation: You are acting as becomes you .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1054b
sections.original: Ea lege hoc adeo faciam ; si facit
sections.translation: On this condition , then , I ’ ll do it ; if he does

sections.label: Section 5.5.1055
sections.translation: that which I think it right he should do .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1055b
sections.translation: Father , I ’ ll do any thing ; command me .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1056
sections.translation: You must take a wife .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1056b
sections.translation: Father —

sections.label: Section 5.5.1056c
sections.translation: I ’ ll hear nothing .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1056d
sections.translation: I ’ ll take it upon myself ; he shall do so .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1057b
sections.translation: I don ’ t hear any thing from him as yet .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1057c
sections.translation: I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 5.5.1057d
sections.translation: Do you hesitate , Clitipho ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.1058
sections.translation: Nay , just as he likes .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1058b
sections.translation: He ’ ll do it all .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1058c
sections.translation: This course , while you are making a beginning , is disagreeable , and while you are unacquainted with it . When you have become acquainted with it , it will become easy .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1059b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do it , father .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1060
sections.original: Gnate mi , ego pol tibi dabo illam lepidam quam tu facile ames ;
sections.translation: My son , upon my honor I ’ ll give you that charming girl , whom you may soon become attached to , the daughter of our neighbor Phanocrata .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1061b
sections.translation: What ! that red-haired girl , with cat ’ s eyes , freckled face , [*] and hooked nose ? I can not , father .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1063
sections.original: Heia , ut elegans est ! credas animum ibi esse .
sections.translation: Heyday ! how nice he is ! You would fancy he had set his mind upon it .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1063b
sections.translation: I ’ ll name another .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1064
sections.original: Quid istic ? quandoquidem ducenda est , egomet habeo propemodum
sections.translation: Why no — since I must marry , I myself have one that I should pretty nearly make choice of .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1065b
sections.translation: Now , son , I commend you .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1065c
sections.translation: The daughter of Archonides here .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1066
sections.translation: I ’ m quite agreeable .
